summary: Large mnmain decomp/matching PR that made src/melee/mn/mnmain.c linkable and matched many main-menu functions, but the author explicitly noted it was not functional yet: pressing Start at the title plays main menu music while the image freezes. The PR added almost 1000 lines to mnmain.c, introduced or filled menu subsystem headers for cross-file calls, adjusted GALE01 split boundaries between mnmain.c and mnmainrule.c, and renamed the sdata byte at 0x804D4B80 to local null_terminator. decomp.dev reported +8024 matched bytes, 22 new mnmain matches, and 4 improved unmatched functions.
what_changed: Replaced many mnmain placeholders with C implementations. decomp.dev listed 22 new 100% matches in main/melee/mn/mnmain, including mn_8022CE6C, mn_8022A5D0, mn_8022D594, mn_8022D34C, mn_8022CC28, mn_8022C304, mn_8022CA54, mn_8022EC18, mn_8022EE84, mn_8022C068, fn_8022C128, mn_8022E978, mn_8022F4CC, mn_8022EA78, mn_8022F410, mn_8022F470, mn_8022F0F0, mn_8022EBDC, mn_8022E950, mn_8022F138, mn_8022F1A8, and mn_8022EFD8.
Improved several still-unmatched mnmain functions according to the bot report: mn_8022D7F4 to 98.49%, mn_8022C7CC to 83.33%, mn_8022D104 to 78.08%, and fn_8022AFEC to 76.05%.

Added many mn subsystem includes to mnmain.c, covering audio and menu destinations such as lbaudio_ax, mncharsel, mncount, mndatadel, mndeflicker, mndiagram, mngallery, mninfo, mninfobonus, mnlanguage, mnmainrule, mnname, mnsnap, mnsound, mnsoundtest, and mnvibration.

Filled previously empty or sparse headers with include guards and placeholder prototypes for menu modules such as mncount.h, mndatadel.h, mndeflicker.h, mninfobonus.h, mnsnap.h, mnvibration.h, mnsound.h, and mnsoundtest.h.

Adjusted concrete prototypes where evidence was available, such as mnDiagram_802437E8(int, int), mnDeflicker_8024A6C4(int), and void-return leaf entry points like mnCount_8025186C(void) and mnInfoBonus_80252F8C(void).
Changed mn_8022F410 call sites in gmtou.c from mn_8022F410(&sp28, 0.4f, &sp24) style ordering to mn_8022F410(&sp28, &sp24, 0.4f), matching the newly established signature.

Refined config/GALE01/splits.txt so mnmain.c text ended at 0x8022F538 instead of 0x8022FEC8, mnmainrule.c text began at 0x8022F538, mnmain sbss/sdata2 shrank, and mnmainrule gained sbss and an adjusted sdata2 range.
Renamed the 0x804D4B80 sdata symbol from mn_804D4B80 to local null_terminator in symbols.txt, and made null_terminator static in mnmain.c.

Reordered and exposed several data objects in mnmain.c to fit linkage/symbol behavior, including MenMain_cam, MenMain_fog, MenMain_lights, mn_804D6BB8, mn_804D6BB5, mn_804D6BB4, StaticModelDesc MenMain* symbols, GXColor data, and repeated declarations of mn_804A04F0 visible in the diff.

Implemented main-menu control-flow handlers using HSD_GObj process setup, GObj destruction/linking, audio cues via lbAudioAx_80024030, and transitions into other menu modules such as mnDiagram_802437E8, mnInfoBonus_80252F8C, mnCount_8025186C, mnHyaku_8024CD64, and gm state helpers.
smart_moves: Made mnmain linkable first even though it was not runtime-correct, allowing matching progress and cross-module signature cleanup to land while preserving a clear functional caveat.
Used split-boundary refinement instead of forcing data into the wrong unit: the author called out a repeated float 0 in sdata2 as evidence that further file split work is needed.
Promoted necessary asset/model descriptors from static to externally linkable symbols where the generated object layout required named StaticModelDesc MenMain* entries.
Centralized repeated menu-selection wraparound code into static inline helpers x2_dec and x2_inc, preserving control flow while making repeated assembly patterns easier to match.

Introduced a small inline mn_8022C068 for point-light color interpolation and reused it in fn_8022C128 and mn_8022C304, matching the compiler's inlining shape rather than only writing high-level equivalent logic.
Updated downstream caller gmtou.c when the mn_8022F410 parameter order became clear, avoiding a stale prototype/call mismatch.
Kept uncertain cross-module functions as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in headers while still adding address-commented prototypes, which improved linkability without overclaiming exact signatures.
decomp_lessons: For large menu files, linkability and matching can advance before runtime functionality; record runtime caveats explicitly so later agents do not mistake a linkable menu for a working one.
Repeated constants in sdata2, especially repeated float 0 values near a file boundary, can be evidence that the current split is too coarse or misassigned.

Menu decomp often requires broad header scaffolding because mnmain dispatches into many sibling menu modules; placeholder prototypes are acceptable when signatures are still unknown, but concrete signatures should be tightened as soon as call sites prove them.
Symbol scope matters for matching: a single-byte null terminator was changed from global mn_804D4B80 to local static null_terminator, aligning both C scope and symbols.txt.

Small type and literal changes can affect matching: examples in the diff include powi(10, arg1) instead of powi(0xA, arg1), count < 11 instead of count < 0xB, unsigned suffix removals, explicit +0.4f, int vs u8 temporary choices, and casts like (u8) var_r25.

When matching HSD object code, explicit object setup order matters: GObj_Create, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, object attachment, and process flag writes are all visible matching constraints.
Inline helpers and pragma dont_inline sections are useful levers in mnmain; the diff contains static inline helpers and a #pragma dont_inline region near late mnmain functions.
Cross-file prototype corrections should be propagated immediately to all callers, as seen with mn_8022F410 in gmtou.c.
naming_conventions: Menu subsystem headers use MELEE_MN_*_H include guards, replacing older guards such as GALE01_24A168 in mndeflicker.h.
Known subsystem functions use CamelCase-ish module prefixes plus addresses, e.g. mnCount_802502CC, mnDataDel_8024E940, mnDeflicker_8024A168, mnInfoBonus_802528F8, and mnSnap_80253184.
Still-unknown or generic local functions retain fn_80xxxxxx names, e.g. fn_802502F0, fn_8024ECCC, fn_80252C50, and fn_8022C128.
Address comments before prototypes remain the project style, e.g. /* 2502CC */ and /* 2437E8 */.
File-local data can use descriptive local names when identified, as with static char null_terminator[1], while unresolved globals retain mn_804D.... or lbl_804D.... names.
Asset/model descriptors keep their archive-style names such as MenMainBack_Top, MenMainPanel_Top, MenMainCursorRl05_Top, MenMain_cam, MenMain_fog, and MenMain_lights.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjusted file splits: mnmain.c .text range changed from 0x802295AC-0x8022FEC8 to 0x802295AC-0x8022F538, with mnmainrule.c starting at 0x8022F538.
Moved sbss/sdata2 ownership between mnmain.c and mnmainrule.c to reflect the newly matched boundary and suspected repeated float 0 issue.
Used static inline helpers for codegen-sensitive fragments: mn_80229A04_dontinline became static inline, mn_8022C068 was inline, and x2_dec/x2_inc captured repeated selection wraparound loops.
Used PAD_STACK declarations and small local padding arrays to control stack layout in functions such as mn_8022C304 and other menu handlers.
Matched boolean/control-flow details with explicit casts and types, including changing var_r25 to int and testing (u8) var_r25 in fn_8022AFEC.
Matched call signatures by reordering mn_8022F410 arguments and updating external callers.
Matched constant spelling and signedness details, including powi(10), 0x10 without U suffixes, +0.4f, +1, and -1 returns.
Left a few remaining placeholders at the end of mnmain, including /// #fn_8022F538, /// #mn_8022FB88, and /// #mn_8022FD18, indicating the PR intentionally did not finish the entire file.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
Author note: mnmain was linkable but not functional; pressing Start at the title screen played main menu music but froze the title image.
Author note: repeated float 0 in sdata2 indicated more file split refinement would be needed.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01 showed matched code increased by 0.21% and +8024 bytes, with 22 new matches and 4 improvements in unmatched mnmain functions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fix mnmain runtime freeze after PR 1844.
Search for additional split refinements around mnmain.c and mnmainrule.c sdata2 repeated float 0.
Search for PRs that complete fn_8022F538, mn_8022FB88, and mn_8022FD18.
Check later headers for concrete replacements of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in mncount, mndatadel, mnsnap, mnsoundtest, and mnvibration.
Search for further corrections to mn_8022F410 signature or callers.
